name: Spiced Vegetable Curry with Pounded Paste
description: A paste-from-scratch vegetable curry. Fixture exercising high placeholder density across all steps.

ingredients.name: dried red chillies, soaked and drained
kaffir lime leaves, central rib removed

steps.title: Pound the curry paste
steps.content: Pound {0001}, {0002}, {0003}, {0004}, {0005}, {0006}, {0007} and {0008} to a coarse paste in a mortar and pestle, working through the ingredients in order from hardest to softest. Add {0009} and continue pounding until smooth and homogeneous. Alternatively, blitz {0001}, {0002}, {0003}, {0004}, {0005}, {0006}, {0007}, {0008} and {0009} in a small food processor with a splash of water — scrape down the sides several times.

steps.title: Fry the paste
steps.content: Heat {0010} in a heavy-based pan over medium heat. Add the curry paste and fry for 3-4 minutes until deeply fragrant, then stir in half of {0011}. Cook, stirring frequently, until the oil splits from the coconut cream — this stage is essential for flavour depth.

steps.title: Build the curry body
steps.content: Add the remaining {0011} and {0012}. Stir in {0013}, {0014} and {0015}. Bring to a gentle simmer.

steps.title: Simmer until tender
steps.content: Simmer uncovered for 15-18 minutes until {0014} is completely tender and {0013} has taken on the curry colour. The sauce should reduce slightly and thicken to coat a spoon.

steps.title: Season and finish
steps.content: Stir in {0017} and {0018}, then taste. Adjust with more {0017} for acidity or more {0018} for sweetness — the balance should be slightly sweet, slightly sour, salty and spicy. Tear in {0016} just before serving.

notes: Paste keeps for a week in the fridge or freezes well in tablespoon portions. Swap aubergine for whatever firm vegetable is to hand — sweet potato, pumpkin or green beans all work.
